This is a wordpress project. When a page loads, it loads in the page title as a classname into the <main> tag. It also loads a custom identifier ($template_name) class used for js and css on each page. This is normally fine. however, we have a situation where the page title matches a custom identifier. This loads more css that what I want to load. My current thought is to refactor the page-*.php and each associated .scss and .js class that uses that class to append “-template” to the $template_name strings. that way there is no conflict and instead each template has a really unique identifier instead. Either follow this process and refactor the project or come up with a similar idea and apply it across the project.
continue

Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Append "-template" Suffix to Template Names

## Problem
Page titles get converted to kebab-case and added as a class to `<main>` (in `header.php`). The `$template_name` also gets added to `<main>`. When they match (e.g., page title "Overview TikTok" → "overview-tiktok" matches `$template_name = 'overview-tiktok'`), unwanted CSS gets loaded.

## Solution
Append "-template" suffix to all `$template_name` values to ensure they never conflict with page title-derived classes.

---

## Changes Required

### 1. Update PHP Templates (38 files)
**Location:** `src/page-*.php`

Change `$template_name` from `'{name}'` to `'{name}-template'` in each file:
- `page-article-hrt-overview.php` → `'article-hrt-overview-template'`
- `page-article-trt.php` → `'article-trt-template'`
- `page-article-trt-new-copy.php` → `'article-trt-new-copy-template'`
- ... (38 templates total)

**Special case:** `page-mens-hrt-aol-forbes.php` adds two class names - both need updating.

### 2. Update HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES Array
**File:** `src/functions.php` (lines 20-60)

Update all 39 entries to have `-template` suffix.

### 3. Rename SCSS Files (39 files)
**Location:** `src/static/scss/pages/`

Rename each file: `{name}.scss` → `{name}-template.scss`

### 4. Update SCSS Selectors (2 files only)
Only these 2 files have explicit `main.{name}` selectors:
- `article-trt-template.scss` (line 11): `main.article-trt {` → `main.article-trt-template {`
- `article-trt-new-copy-template.scss` (line 14): `main.article-trt-new-copy {` → `main.article-trt-new-copy-template {`

### 5. Rename JS Bundle Files (39 files)
**Location:** `src/static/js/pages/`

Rename each file: `{name}.bundle.js` → `{name}-template.bundle.js`

### 6. Clean Old Compiled Files & Rebuild
```bash
rm -f src/static/css/pages/*.css src/static/css/pages/*.map
rm -f src/static/js/dist/pages/*.js
yarn build:assets
```

---

## Execution Order

1. Rename SCSS files (git mv for proper tracking)
2. Rename JS bundle files (git mv for proper tracking)
3. Update SCSS selectors in the 2 affected files
4. Update `$template_name` in all PHP templates
5. Update `HONE_PAGE_TEMPLATES` array in functions.php
6. Clean old compiled files
7. Run `yarn build:assets`

---

## File Counts
- 38 PHP templates to update
- 1 functions.php array to update (39 entries)
- 39 SCSS files to rename
- 2 SCSS files to edit selectors
- 39 JS bundle files to rename

---

## Verification
1. Run `yarn build:assets` - should complete without errors
2. Verify new CSS files exist in `src/static/css/pages/` with `-template` suffix
3. Verify new JS files exist in `src/static/js/dist/pages/` with `-template` suffix
4. Verify old files are gone
5. Test a page in browser - inspect `<main>` class to confirm template class has `-template` suffix
